title: Isaiah 61
subtitle: The Spirit of the LORD Is on Me: The Nazareth Manifesto

sections.header: Verses 1–6 — “Annointed to Proclaim Good News to the Poor”

heb.paragraph: "The Spirit of the Sovereign LORD is on me, because the LORD has anointed (māshaḥ) me to proclaim good news to the poor. He has sent me to bind up the broken-hearted, to proclaim freedom for the captives and release from darkness for the prisoners, to proclaim the year of the LORD’s favour" (vv.1–2). Jesus reads THIS text in the Nazareth synagogue, rolls up the scroll, and says: "Today this scripture is fulfilled in your hearing" (Luke 4:18–21). He stops MID-SENTENCE — at "the year of the LORD’s favour" — omitting "and the day of vengeance of our God" (v.2b). The omission is the sermon: favour now, vengeance later. "They will be called oaks of righteousness, a planting of the LORD for the display of his splendour" (v.3). Ruined cities rebuilt (v.4). Foreigners will shepherd your flocks (v.5). "You will be called priests of the LORD" (v.6).

notes.note: "The Spirit of the LORD is on me, because the LORD has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor." Luke 4:18–21: Jesus inaugural sermon. He claims this text for himself. The anointing is messianic: māshaḥ gives us the word Messiah.

"The year of the LORD’s favour and the day of vengeance of our God." Jesus reads "favour" and stops. The year of favour is present; the day of vengeance is future. The comma between them is the church age.
"They will be called oaks of righteousness, a planting of the LORD." The redeemed as trees: rooted, strong, displaying God’s splendour. The garden-imagery of 35:1 and 55:12 culminates here.
"You will be called priests of the LORD." The entire people become priests. 1 Peter 2:9: "a royal priesthood." The servant makes a priestly nation.

sections.header: Verses 7–11 — “As the Earth Brings Forth Its Sprouts”

heb.paragraph: "For I, the LORD, love justice; I hate robbery and wrongdoing. In my faithfulness I will reward my people and make an everlasting covenant with them" (v.8). Double portion instead of shame; everlasting joy instead of disgrace (v.7). "I delight greatly in the LORD. He has clothed me with garments of salvation and arrayed me in a robe of his righteousness (ṣədāqâ)" (v.10). Salvation as clothing: garments of salvation, robe of righteousness. "As a bridegroom adorns his head like a priest, and as a bride adorns herself with her jewels" (v.10). "As the earth brings forth its sprouts, so the Sovereign LORD will make righteousness and praise spring up before all nations" (v.11).

notes.note: "Instead of your shame you will receive a double portion. I will make an everlasting covenant with them." Double for shame. Everlasting covenant. The reversal is permanent, not temporary.

"He has clothed me with garments of salvation and arrayed me in a robe of righteousness, as a bridegroom, as a bride." The wedding imagery: salvation is wedding-clothes. The Messiah is both groom and priest.
"As the earth brings forth its sprouts, so the LORD will make righteousness spring up before all nations." Righteousness as organic growth: not imposed but sprouting. All nations see it.

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVTheir descendants will be known among the nations and their offspring among the peoples. All who see them will acknowledge that they are a people the Lord has blessed.”ESVTheir offspring shall be known among the nations, and their descendants in the midst of the peoples; all who see them shall acknowledge them, that they are an offspring the LORD has blessed.NIVAnd you will be called priests of the Lord, you will be named ministers of our God. You will feed on the wealth of nations, and in their riches you will boast.ESVbut you shall be called the priests of the LORD; they shall speak of you as the ministers of our God; you shall eat the wealth of the nations, and in their glory you shall boast.

rec.text: ""The Spirit of the LORD is on me, because the LORD has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor." Luke 4:18–21: Jesus inaugural sermon. He claims this text for himself. The anointing is messianic…"

rows.label: Garments of Salvation — 61:7–11
rows.text: Double for shame. "GARMENTS OF SALVATION, robe of RIGHTEOUSNESS." Bridegroom and bride. Righteousness SPRINGS UP before all nations.

hebtext.note: "The Spirit of the Sovereign LORD is on me, because the LORD has anointed (māshaḥ) me to proclaim good news to the poor. He has sent me to bind up the broken-hearted, to proclaim freedom for the captives and release from darkness for the prisoners, to proclaim the year of the LORD’s favour" (vv.1–2). Jesus reads THIS text in the Nazareth synagogue, rolls up the scroll, and says: "Today this scripture is fulfilled in your hearing" (Luke 4:18–21). He stops MID-SENTENCE — at "the year of the LORD’s favour" — omitting "and the day of vengeance of our God" (v.2b). The omission is the sermon: favour now, vengeance later. "They will be called oaks of righteousness, a planting of the LORD for the display of his splendour" (v.3). Ruined cities rebuilt (v.4). Foreigners will shepherd your flocks (v.5). "You will be called priests of the LORD" (v.6).

"For I, the LORD, love justice; I hate robbery and wrongdoing. In my faithfulness I will reward my people and make an everlasting covenant with them" (v.8). Double portion instead of shame; everlasting joy instead of disgrace (v.7). "I delight greatly in the LORD. He has clothed me with garments of salvation and arrayed me in a robe of his righteousness (ṣədāqâ)" (v.10). Salvation as clothing: garments of salvation, robe of righteousness. "As a bridegroom adorns his head like a priest, and as a bride adorns herself with her jewels" (v.10). "As the earth brings forth its sprouts, so the Sovereign LORD will make righteousness and praise spring up before all nations" (v.11).

themes.note: Prophecy AND Mercy peak at 10: Luke 4:18–21 — Jesus inaugural sermon. The comma between favour and vengeance IS the church age. Priestly nation (1 Pet 2:9). Mission at 9: all nations.
